translations.translation:
Guo Yuan (Zini)
國淵 (子尼)
Lifespan Unlisted
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
byZL181
onAugust 30th, 2025

Guo Yuan, styledZini, was fromGai[蓋],Le’an[樂安].
He treatedZheng Xuanas a teacher.ALater, he and others, includingBing YuanandGuan Ning, fled the chaos toLiaodong[遼東].B
TheSeparate Biography of[Zheng] Xuanstates:[Guo] Yuaninitially did not have a famed reputation.[Zheng] Xuanpraised him, saying, “GuoZiniis a magnificent talent! I have witnessed such a person who will certainly become a critical worker for the state!”
TheBook of WeibyWang Chenstates:[Guo] Yuanwas eager in studying and he was fond of the ancients. When he was inLiaodong[遼東], he would often teach on mountains and on cliffs. Many scholars respected and admired him. From this, he gained a famed reputation.
When he returned to his former lands, theGrand Progenitorrecruited him to serve asbureaucratic clerkunder theExcellency of Works. Each time he was in the government court for a discussion, he would always speak candidly and with a forthright expression. Whenever he withdrew, he was without any private ties.
TheGrand Progenitorwanted to expand the Field Garrison system and tasked[Guo] Yuanwith directing the work.[Guo] Yuanwould frequently explain downsides and benefits, he would examine the land before settling commoners, and he would install officials based on the count of commoners. He made the methods of performance evaluation clear. Within five years, the granaries were filled. The common people competed with each other in order to motivate themselves to do their jobs with gusto.
When theGrand Progenitorinvaded theGuanzhong Basin[關中], he had[Guo] Yuanserve asChief Clerk who Resides in Bureaus, having him manage matters that he left behind.Tian YinandSu Borebelled inHejian[河間]. When[Tian] Yinand the others were defeated, their remaining comrades were all to be executed.[Guo] Yuanbelieved that they were not the orchestrators of the evil, and thus requested that they should not be punished. TheGrand Progenitorheeded him. It was due to[Guo] Yuanthat over one thousand people were able to live. In documents pertaining to defeating rebels, one [decapitated head] was made out to be ten [heads] in the past. However, when[Guo] Yuanreported the heads, he did so according to the actual count. When theGrand Progenitorasked him for his reasons,[Guo] Yuansaid, “In campaigns against outside enemies, the number of the beheaded and the captured is exaggerated. The intention is to inflate military glory to show the common people compliance.Hejian[河間] is located within a fiefdom, yet[Tian] Yinand the others rebelled. Even though I achieved a victory with glory, I feel ashamed.” TheGrand Progenitorwas very delighted and promoted him toGrand AdministratorofWei[魏郡太守].
At the time, there was a person who submitted a book that contained slanderous and defamatory statements.1TheGrand Progenitorhated it and wanted to ascertain who the man was.[Guo] Yuanasked to keep the original book and to not make any announcements. In the book were many citations to theRhapsody of the Two Capitals.2[Guo] Yuangave this command to theOfficer of Merit:•••
The letter was likely submitted by being placed inside a “report container” [缿筒], where one can easily insert an anonymous letter through a small opening.↩
This was a rhapsody written by Zhang Heng [張衡], split into two parts for the western capital and the eastern capital. The rhapsody details the opulence of the western capital, Chang’an [長安], and the humility of the eastern capital, Luoyang [洛陽], going into minute detail about daily life and landmarks.↩
“This commandery is vast and presently where thecapital cityis, and yet there are few knowledgeable people. This letter can educate the youth; I want to send them to such a teacher!”
TheOfficer of Meritsent three people. Before they were sent, he led them into a meeting and instructed them, “Your learning is not good enough yet. TheRhapsody of the Two Capitalsis a book of diverse subjects. Many people in the world have overlooked it and so there is a scarcity of those who can teach it. You should seek someone who can peruse it and receive instructions from them.” He also secretly shared his intentions. After ten days or so, they found someone who could peruse it, and thus went to be educated by him. The officials then asked him to make annotations. They compared it with the book, and it was by the same hands of the person who distributed the book. They arrested him, interrogated him, and was provided with the context.
He was promoted toMinister Chamberlain. When he resided in the position ofMinister, he donned cheap clothes and vegetarian meals. He donated his salary to his acquaintances and his kin. Through his respectfulness and frugality, he checked himself. He died inoffice.C
TheBook of WeibyWang Chenstates: TheGrand Progenitorappointed his son,[Guo] Tai, asGentleman.
